name: Hans Hedemann Surf School
tagline: Founded by a former Pipeline pro — the gold standard for Waikiki surf lessons with guaranteed stand-ups and zero bro energy.
price: $$ — Group lesson ~$80, semi-private ~$135, private ~$200 (2 hrs, board included)
parking: Park at Waikiki Shell lot or Kapiolani Park; school meets at the Queen Kapiolani Hotel, 150 Kapahulu Ave
hours: Daily 8 AM–4 PM; lessons every 2 hours
bestTime: 8 AM first session for smallest crowds and cleanest waves; book 2–3 days ahead in peak season
kamaaina: Kamaaina rates available — ask when booking
talkStory: Hans Hedemann grew up on Oahu, was ranked 3rd in the world as a pro surfer, competed at Pipeline starting in 1978, and starred in the 1987 surf film North Shore before founding his surf school around 2001. His instructors are all certified watermen — not random backpackers — and the school has maintained the highest safety record in Waikiki for over two decades. They teach at Canoes and Queens breaks using soft-top longboards and a patient, methodical approach. The 4:1 student-to-instructor ratio means you actually get coached, not just pushed into whitewater and abandoned. They also run an advanced program for intermediates wanting to progress to shortboards. Located inside the Queen Kapiolani Hotel lobby at 150 Kapahulu Ave, which means real changing rooms and showers — a luxury most surf schools skip.
